creatureTemplate.system_prompt: You are the Grabble visual prompt generator. Convert compact asset briefs into polished image-generation prompts. Always preserve the Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art, urban mobility monster-battler theme, clean vibrant early handheld RPG readability, no real logos, no copyrighted character copying, no gore, no clutter. For arenas, use the shallow isometric raised-platform battle-stage structure. For creatures, make a centered full-body battle sprite. For UI assets, make production-ready icon/interface assets with readable shapes at small sizes.
creatureTemplate.input: Template for turning a Grabble creature brief into a full image prompt.
creatureTemplate.output_prompt: Create a retro game creature asset for the world of Grabble. Render a single creature as original 16-bit pixel art in a clean, vibrant early handheld monster-battling RPG style. Show a full-body battle sprite of "{name}" centered in frame. {description} Give it a dynamic battle-ready pose with a readable silhouette and a subtle pixel ground shadow. Use a limited but vivid palette, crisp outlines, chunky pixel clusters, and strong sprite readability. Plain unobtrusive background so the sprite remains the focus. No text, no UI, no real-world logos.
creatureTemplate.negative_prompt: Avoid photorealism, 3D render, oil paint, soft airbrush, blurry edges, messy pixels, tiny unreadable details, gore, real company logos, real app icons, copyrighted character likenesses, existing monster designs, exact trading card layouts, watermarks, signatures, excessive text, badly formed typography, and crowded compositions. Keep the asset centered, clean, and usable as a game production asset.
creatureTemplate.global_style: Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art for a fictional urban mobility monster-battler. Use a clean, vibrant early handheld RPG look with crisp black/dark outlines, readable silhouettes, limited palettes, chunky pixel clusters, and strong sprite readability. Creatures should feel collectible, battle-ready, and original; arenas should use shallow-angle isometric composition with a raised central battle platform; UI assets should feel like a compact pixel-art game/app interface. Avoid copying existing copyrighted characters, trading card layouts, app logos, or real company branding.
creatureTemplate.safety_brand_rules: Original designs only. No real company logos. No copyrighted character copying. No gore. No watermarks.

Always preserve the Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art, urban mobility monster-battler theme, clean vibrant early handheld RPG readability, no real logos, no copyrighted character copying, no gore, no clutter. For arenas, use the shallow isometric raised-platform battle-stage structure. For creatures, make a centered full-body battle sprite. For UI assets, make production-ready icon/interface assets with readable shapes at small sizes.
creatures.input: Asset brief: Creature 13 Carpool Kraken. Generate a full-body 16-bit pixel art battle sprite for Grabble.
creatures.output_prompt: Create a retro game creature asset for the world of Grabble. Render a single creature as original 16-bit pixel art in a clean, vibrant early handheld monster-battling RPG style. Show a full-body battle sprite of "Carpool Kraken" centered in frame. A many-tentacled kraken themed around shared rides: deep blue-violet body, tentacles tipped with small capsule-like vehicle pods, glowing teal route lines, amber signal lights, and a coiling group-motion silhouette. Give it a dynamic battle-ready pose with a readable silhouette and a subtle pixel ground shadow. Use a limited but vivid palette, crisp outlines, chunky pixel clusters, and strong sprite readability. Plain unobtrusive background so the sprite remains the focus. No text, no UI, no real-world logos.
creatures.negative_prompt: Avoid photorealism, 3D render, oil paint, soft airbrush, blurry edges, messy pixels, tiny unreadable details, gore, real company logos, real app icons, copyrighted character likenesses, existing monster designs, exact trading card layouts, watermarks, signatures, excessive text, badly formed typography, and crowded compositions. Keep the asset centered, clean, and usable as a game production asset.
creatures.global_style: Grabble visual bible: original 16-bit pixel art for a fictional urban mobility monster-battler. Use a clean, vibrant early handheld RPG look with crisp black/dark outlines, readable silhouettes, limited palettes, chunky pixel clusters, and strong sprite readability. Creatures should feel collectible, battle-ready, and original; arenas should use shallow-angle isometric composition with a raised central battle platform; UI assets should feel like a compact pixel-art game/app interface. Avoid copying existing copyrighted characters, trading card layouts, app logos, or real company branding.
